evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Count app I want to mark each scanned barcode with a status (e.g. expired) and show it automatically as items are scanned. File: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill implements a BarcodeCountStatusProvider, registers it via barcodeCountView.setStatusProvider(...), and sets barcodeCountView.shouldShowStatusIconsOnScan = true. In onStatusRequested it builds a BarcodeCountStatusItem per barcode via BarcodeCountStatusItem.create(trackedBarcode, BarcodeCountStatus.X), wraps them in BarcodeCountStatusResultSuccess.create(...), and delivers it via callback.onStatusReady(...).

assertions.text: A BarcodeCountStatusProvider is implemented and registered via barcodeCountView.setStatusProvider(...)
onStatusRequested(barcodes, callback) builds one BarcodeCountStatusItem per barcode via BarcodeCountStatusItem.create(trackedBarcode, status)
The status value is a BarcodeCountStatus enum value (e.g. BarcodeCountStatus.EXPIRED) — Android uses the fixed enum, not a custom icon builder
The result is built with BarcodeCountStatusResultSuccess.create(...) and delivered via callback.onStatusReady(...)
barcodeCountView.shouldShowStatusIconsOnScan = true is set so statuses appear automatically on scan

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Count app, only show the per-barcode statuses when the user taps the status-mode button, not automatically. File: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill sets barcodeCountView.shouldShowStatusModeButton = true and leaves shouldShowStatusIconsOnScan at its default (false), in addition to registering a status provider.

assertions.text: shouldShowStatusIconsOnScan is NOT set to true (left at its default false) so the button flow is used
A BarcodeCountStatusProvider is still registered via setStatusProvider so there are statuses to show

evals.prompt: My MatrixScan Count statuses come from a backend call that can fail. Show me how to handle the success and the failure cases in the status provider. File: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill shows resolving statuses asynchronously and delivering the appropriate result type via callback.onStatusReady(...): BarcodeCountStatusResultSuccess.create(...) on success, and BarcodeCountStatusResultError.create(...) or BarcodeCountStatusResultAbort.create(...) on failure.

assertions.text: On success it delivers BarcodeCountStatusResultSuccess.create(statusItems, ...) via callback.onStatusReady(...)
On failure it delivers BarcodeCountStatusResultError.create(...) and/or BarcodeCountStatusResultAbort.create(...)
It uses the static create(...) factories on the result classes — not constructors
callback.onStatusReady(...) is called exactly once per onStatusRequested invocation (semantic)
